gym.notes: Good session. The discipline of the routine is its own argument. Twenty-five years. There is something clarifying about a practice that predates every professional identity I have had.

atlantic_column.status: Week 8 draft — on the happiness research around friendship vs. income
atlantic_column.notes: Worried it reads too much like a listicle. The data is strong but the writing has to do more than report it. Sent draft to Elena at HBS for a read. She said the middle section needed a harder claim. She was right. Revised.

book.notes: Wrote 1,200 words on the chapter about fluid vs. crystallized intelligence. The autobiographical frame is working but I'm using 'I' more than I intended. Need to decide whether this book has a first-person spine or not.
book.concern: I keep writing around the AEI chapter instead of into it. The reader will see this.

harvard.notes: Managing Happiness course went well today. The MBA students are more skeptical than Kennedy School students — they keep asking 'but how do you measure it.' Good question. I like teaching people who push back.
harvard.difference_from_aic: I miss the institutional momentum of AEI. I don't miss what the momentum was pointed at. This is a useful distinction.

ester.notes: We said the rosary and then talked for an hour after. She asked when I was going to write the AEI chapter honestly. I said soon. She nodded in the way that means she will ask again.
ester.observation: She has been asking the right questions for thirty years. I have not always been answering them.

friendship_goal.notes: Dinner with Paul and Maria on Saturday. Confirmed. The first time I've had dinner with friends who are not professional contacts in three months. This is not something I would have noticed two years ago.

wins: Dinner with Paul and Maria confirmed
at_risk: AEI chapter — still routing around it
note: The students today asked whether happiness is something you achieve or something you practice. I gave them the research answer. The honest answer is that I am still figuring out the difference.
